nodes.x: After this there was a feast of the Jews; and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
¶ And the Jews’ passover was at hand, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem,
And when he was twelve years old, they went up to Jerusalem after the custom of the feast.
¶ On the next day much people that were come to the feast, when they heard that Jesus was coming to Jerusalem,
¶ Now about the midst of the feast Jesus went up into the temple, and taught.
And Jesus entered into Jerusalem, and into the temple: and when he had looked round about upon all things, and now the eventide was come, he went out unto Bethany with the twelve.
Now there is at Jerusalem by the sheep market a pool, which is called in the Hebrew tongue Bethesda, having five porches.

I found plenty of water there for baptizing, besides a surplus quantity sent off in n limpid stream as a grateful tribute to the thirsty plants of tho gardens in the valley. The pool of Ifethsada, which had five porches, yet remains in the city* but in a dilapidated state, there being plenty of water to meet the demands of the city of a better quality, and more convenient — this vast reservoir is…

It was on the right bank of this stream, at a choice spot upon a rich table land, traversed by a great company of exhaustlees streams, falling from the highlands, that the Pioneer band of Mormons, coming out of the mountains in the night, pitched their first camp in the Valley, and consecrated the ground. Curiously enough, this very spot proved the most favourable arte for their chief settlement,…

And then cometh the New Jerusalem; and blessed are they who dwell therein, for it is they whose garments are white through the blood of the Lamb; and they are they who are numbered among the remnant of the seed of Joseph, who were of the house of Israel.
In these lay a great multitude of impotent folk, of blind, halt, withered, waiting for the moving of the water.

The Moving of the Water By Elder Boyd K. Packer Of the Quorum of the Twelve Apostles “Now there is at Jerusalem by the sheep market a pool, which is called in the Hebrew tongue Bethesda, having five porches. “In these lay a great multitude of impotent folk, of blind, halt, withered, waiting for the moving of the water. “For an angel went down at a certain season into the pool, and troubled the…
And art confident that thou thyself art a guide of the blind, a light of them which are in darkness,
Hungry and thirsty, their soul fainted in them.
The blind receive their sight, and the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, and the deaf hear, the dead are raised up, and the poor have the gospel preached to them.
The impotent man answered him, Sir, I have no man, when the water is troubled, to put me into the pool: but while I am coming, another steppeth down before me.
For an angel went down at a certain season into the pool, and troubled the water: whosoever then first after the troubling of the water stepped in was made whole of whatsoever disease he had.
And the third angel poured out his vial upon the rivers and fountains of waters; and they became blood.
And the second angel poured out his vial upon the sea; and it became as the blood of a dead man: and every living soul died in the sea.
And the third angel sounded, and there fell a great star from heaven, burning as it were a lamp, and it fell upon the third part of the rivers, and upon the fountains of waters;
In all their affliction he was afflicted, and the angel of his presence saved them: in his love and in his pity he redeemed them; and he bare them, and carried them all the days of old.
And a certain man was there, which had an infirmity thirty and eight years.
And now it came to pass, if there was no mistake made by this man in the reckoning of our time, the thirty and third year had passed away;
And it came to pass that in this year there were many people who went forth into the land northward. And thus ended the thirty and eighth year.

And thus did the thirty and eighth year pass away, and also the thirty and ninth, and forty and first, and the forty and second, yea, even until forty and nine years had passed away, and also the fifty and first, and the fifty and second; yea, and even until fifty and nine years had passed away.
And thirty years had passed away from the time we left Jerusalem.
And it came to pass that two hundred and thirty and eight years had passed away—after the manner of wars, and contentions, and dissensions, for the space of much of the time.
When Jesus saw him lie, and knew that he had been now a long time in that case, he saith unto him, Wilt thou be made whole?
And he said unto him, Arise, go thy way: thy faith hath made thee whole.
Jesus heard that they had cast him out; and when he had found him, he said unto him, Dost thou believe on the Son of God?

And he said unto me: Because of thy faith in Christ, whom thou hast never before heard nor seen. And many years pass away before he shall manifest himself in the flesh; wherefore, go to, thy faith hath made thee whole.
Then came the Jews round about him, and said unto him, How long dost thou make us to doubt? If thou be the Christ, tell us plainly.
And Jesus said unto him, Go thy way; thy faith hath made thee whole. And immediately he received his sight, and followed Jesus in the way.
The impotent man answered him, Sir, I have no man, when the water is troubled, to put me into the pool: but while I am coming, another steppeth down before me.
In these lay a great multitude of impotent folk, of blind, halt, withered, waiting for the moving of the water.
The woman saith unto him, Sir, give me this water, that I thirst not, neither come hither to draw.
The woman saith unto him, Sir, thou hast nothing to draw with, and the well is deep: from whence then hast thou that living water?
Then asked they him, What man is that which said unto thee, Take up thy bed, and walk?
Jesus saith unto him, Rise, take up thy bed, and walk.
I say unto thee, Arise, and take up thy bed, and go thy way into thine house.
Jesus saith unto him, If I will that he tarry till I come, what is that to thee? follow thou me.
Wherefore, stand ye in holy places, and be not moved, until the day of the Lord come; for behold, it cometh quickly, saith the Lord. Amen.
Jesus saith unto him, Go thy way; thy son liveth. And the man believed the word that Jesus had spoken unto him, and he went his way.
And immediately the man was made whole, and took up his bed, and walked: and on the same day was the sabbath.
And that day was the preparation, and the sabbath drew on.
And it was the sabbath day when Jesus made the clay, and opened his eyes.
And he said unto them, The sabbath was made for man, and not man for the sabbath:

In  the  evening  of  the  same  day,  when  the  sun  had  set, 
and  therefore  after  the  Sabbath  had  passed,**  the  people 
flocked  about  Him,  bringing  their  afflicted  friends  and  kin-
¶ The Jews therefore said unto him that was cured, It is the sabbath day: it is not lawful for thee to carry thy bed.
¶ And, behold, there was a man which had his hand withered. And they asked him, saying, Is it lawful to heal on the sabbath days? that they might accuse him.
And immediately the man was made whole, and took up his bed, and walked: and on the same day was the sabbath.
And the Pharisees said unto him, Behold, why do they on the sabbath day that which is not lawful?
He answered them, He that made me whole, the same said unto me, Take up thy bed, and walk.
And he answered and said unto them, I will also ask you one thing; and answer me:
Then I answered them, The word of the Lord came unto me, saying,
And he answered and said unto them, My mother and my brethren are these which hear the word of God, and do it.
And he answered me and said, Knowest thou not what these be? And I said, No, my lord.
Then asked they him, What man is that which said unto thee, Take up thy bed, and walk?
Jesus saith unto him, Rise, take up thy bed, and walk.
He answered them, He that made me whole, the same said unto me, Take up thy bed, and walk.
And he said unto them, What manner of man was he which came up to meet you, and told you these words?
Again he said unto her, Stand in the door of the tent, and it shall be, when any man doth come and inquire of thee, and say, Is there any man here? that thou shalt say, No.
And he said unto me, Son of man, stand upon thy feet, and I will speak unto thee.
And he that was healed wist not who it was: for Jesus had conveyed himself away, a multitude being in that place.
But when Jesus knew it, he withdrew himself from thence: and great multitudes followed him, and he healed them all;
And great multitudes followed him; and he healed them there.
And Jesus went forth, and saw a great multitude, and was moved with compassion toward them, and he healed their sick.
And the people, when they knew it, followed him: and he received them, and spake unto them of the kingdom of God, and healed them that had need of healing.
And the whole multitude sought to touch him: for there went virtue out of him, and healed them all.
Afterward Jesus findeth him in the temple, and said unto him, Behold, thou art made whole: sin no more, lest a worse thing come unto thee.
And saying, Thou that destroyest the temple, and buildest it in three days, save thyself. If thou be the Son of God, come down from the cross.
And he brought him to Jerusalem, and set him on a pinnacle of the temple, and said unto him, If thou be the Son of God, cast thyself down from hence:

Then said he unto me, This is the curse that goeth forth over the face of the whole earth: for every one that stealeth shall be cut off as on this side according to it; and every one that sweareth shall be cut off as on that side according to it.

Then Jesus beholding him loved him, and said unto him, One thing thou lackest: go thy way, sell whatsoever thou hast, and give to the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in heaven: and come, take up the cross, and follow me.
The man departed, and told the Jews that it was Jesus, which had made him whole.
Because that by reason of him many of the Jews went away, and believed on Jesus.
And when he heard of Jesus, he sent unto him the elders of the Jews, beseeching him that he would come and heal his servant.
And when he had said these words, the Jews departed, and had great reasoning among themselves.
For he mightily convinced the Jews, and that publickly, shewing by the scriptures that Jesus was Christ.
Then the band and the captain and officers of the Jews took Jesus, and bound him,
And therefore did the Jews persecute Jesus, and sought to slay him, because he had done these things on the sabbath day.
Therefore the Jews sought the more to kill him, because he not only had broken the sabbath, but said also that God was his Father, making himself equal with God.
And the scribes and Pharisees watched him, whether he would heal on the sabbath day; that they might find an accusation against him.
And he reasoned in the synagogue every sabbath, and persuaded the Jews and the Greeks.
And Paul, as his manner was, went in unto them, and three sabbath days reasoned with them out of the scriptures,
For they that dwell at Jerusalem, and their rulers, because they knew him not, nor yet the voices of the prophets which are read every sabbath day, they have fulfilled them in condemning him.
¶ But Jesus answered them, My Father worketh hitherto, and I work.
Jesus answered them, I told you, and ye believed not: the works that I do in my Father’s name, they bear witness of me.
Jesus answered them, and said, My doctrine is not mine, but his that sent me.
Jesus answered them, Many good works have I shewed you from my Father; for which of those works do ye stone me?
Jesus answered and said unto them, This is the work of God, that ye believe on him whom he hath sent.
Jesus answered and said unto them, I have done one work, and ye all marvel.
Therefore the Jews sought the more to kill him, because he not only had broken the sabbath, but said also that God was his Father, making himself equal with God.
And therefore did the Jews persecute Jesus, and sought to slay him, because he had done these things on the sabbath day.
¶ And after that many days were fulfilled, the Jews took counsel to kill him:
The Jews answered him, We have a law, and by our law he ought to die, because he made himself the Son of God.
Then answered Jesus and said unto them, Verily, verily, I say unto you, The Son can do nothing of himself, but what he seeth the Father do: for what things soever he doeth, these also doeth the Son…
Then said Jesus unto them, When ye have lifted up the Son of man, then shall ye know that I am he, and that I do nothing of myself; but as my Father hath taught me, I speak these things.
And when he had spoken unto them, he turned himself unto the three, and said unto them: What will ye that I should do unto you, when I am gone unto the Father?

He, being in the truth and living by the truth; therefore he is now to us, “the way, the truth, and the life.” Overcoming all things He was entitled to inherit all things, and all that the Father hath was given unto Him. And we read: “The Son can do nothing of himself, but what he seeth the Father do: for what things soever he doeth, these also doeth the Son likewise.” (John v, 19.)” As the…

the glory which they have, never could be enjoyed by them; for it requires them to be precisely what they are in order to enjoy it: and if the Savior gives this glory to any others, he must do it in the very way set forth in his prayer to his Father: by making them one with him, as he and the Father are one.— In so doing he would give them the glory which the Father has given him; and when his…
Then said they unto him, Who art thou? And Jesus saith unto them, Even the same that I said unto you from the beginning.
For the Father loveth the Son, and sheweth him all things that himself doeth: and he will shew him greater works than these, that ye may marvel.

For in that day, for my sake shall the Father work a work, which shall be a great and a marvelous work among them; and there shall be among them those who will not believe it, although a man shall declare it unto them.

1. Now behold, a marvelous work is about to come forth among the
    children of men;

tions, so will those who are made like him be able to do the same. There are many other things that would be profitable to dwell upon in discussing the increased capacities and powers of man in his future state besides the physical qualities I have spoken of. There is his increased knowledge and the proportionate increase of power that will accompany it; the great creative principle, the…

For behold, this is written for the intent that ye may believe that; and if ye believe that ye will believe this also; and if ye believe this ye will know concerning your fathers, and also the marvelous works which were wrought by the power of God among them.
For as the Father raiseth up the dead, and quickeneth them; even so the Son quickeneth whom he will.
(As it is written, I have made thee a father of many nations,) before him whom he believed, even God, who quickeneth the dead, and calleth those things which be not as though they were.
And the redemption of the soul is through him that quickeneth all things, in whose bosom it is decreed that the poor and the meek of the earth shall inherit it.
But he shall acknowledge the son of the hated for the firstborn, by giving him a double portion of all that he hath: for he is the beginning of his strength; the right of the firstborn is his.
And to wait for his Son from heaven, whom he raised from the dead, even Jesus, which delivered us from the wrath to come.
He that delicately bringeth up his servant from a child shall have him become his son at the length.
For the Father judgeth no man, but hath committed all judgment unto the Son:
And hath given him authority to execute judgment also, because he is the Son of man.

That hath taken off his hand from the poor, that hath not received usury nor increase, hath executed my judgments, hath walked in my statutes; he shall not die for the iniquity of his father, he shall surely live.
¶ Yet say ye, Why? doth not the son bear the iniquity of the father? When the son hath done that which is lawful and right, and hath kept all my statutes, and hath done them, he shall surely live.
He that hath not given forth upon usury, neither hath taken any increase, that hath withdrawn his hand from iniquity, hath executed true judgment between man and man,
That all men should honour the Son, even as they honour the Father. He that honoureth not the Son honoureth not the Father which hath sent him.
His sons come to honour, and he knoweth it not; and they are brought low, but he perceiveth it not of them.
For he received from God the Father honour and glory, when there came such a voice to him from the excellent glory, This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.
For God commanded, saying, Honour thy father and mother: and, He that curseth father or mother, let him die the death.
But last of all he sent unto them his son, saying, They will reverence my son.
And honour not his father or his mother, he shall be free. Thus have ye made the commandment of God of none effect by your tradition.
Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that heareth my word, and believeth on him that sent me, hath everlasting life, and shall not come into condemnation; but is passed from death unto life.
Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that believeth on me hath everlasting life.

Yea, verily I say unto you, if ye will come unto me ye shall have eternal life. Behold, mine arm of mercy is extended towards you, and whosoever will come, him will I receive; and blessed are those who come unto me.
Incline your ear, and come unto me: hear, and your soul shall live; and I will make an everlasting covenant with you, even the sure mercies of David.
Verily, verily, I say unto you, If a man keep my saying, he shall never see death.
Verily, verily, I say unto you, The hour is coming, and now is, when the dead shall hear the voice of the Son of God: and they that hear shall live.
And the servants of God shall go forth, saying with a loud voice: Fear God and give glory to him, for the hour of his judgment is come;
And it shall come to pass in that day, I will hear, saith the Lord, I will hear the heavens, and they shall hear the earth;
And again I say, hearken unto my voice, lest death shall overtake you; in an hour when ye think not the summer shall be past, and the harvest ended, and your souls not saved.
And what I say unto one, I say unto all men; watch, therefore, for you know not at what hour your Lord doth come.
For as the Father hath life in himself; so hath he given to the Son to have life in himself;
He that hath the Son hath life; and he that hath not the Son of God hath not life.
And hath given him authority to execute judgment also, because he is the Son of man.
For the Father judgeth no man, but hath committed all judgment unto the Son:
And it came to pass that he did execute judgment in righteousness; and he did spread his kingdom upon all the face of the land, for the people had become exceedingly numerous.
And his sons did unto him according as he commanded them:
Marvel not at this: for the hour is coming, in the which all that are in the graves shall hear his voice,
Verily, verily, I say unto you, The hour is coming, and now is, when the dead shall hear the voice of the Son of God: and they that hear shall live.

And because of my mourning and lamentation ye have gathered yourselves together, and do marvel; yea, and ye have great need to marvel; yea, ye ought to marvel because ye are given away that the devil has got so great hold upon your hearts.
For the voice of him that reproacheth and blasphemeth; by reason of the enemy and avenger.
Speaking of the resurrection of the dead, concerning those who shall hear the voice of the Son of Man:
And shall come forth; they that have done good, unto the resurrection of life; and they that have done evil, unto the resurrection of damnation.
And shall come forth; they who have done good, in the resurrection of the just; and they who have done evil, in the resurrection of the unjust.

If they be good, to the resurrection of endless life and happiness; and if they be evil, to the resurrection of endless damnation, being delivered up to the devil, who hath subjected them, which is damnation—
And, in fine, wo unto all those who die in their sins; for they shall return to God, and behold his face, and remain in their sins.
These are they who shall come forth in the resurrection of the just.
I can of mine own self do nothing: as I hear, I judge: and my judgment is just; because I seek not mine own will, but the will of the Father which hath sent me.
For I came down from heaven, not to do mine own will, but the will of him that sent me.
But with me it is a very small thing that I should be judged of you, or of man’s judgment: yea, I judge not mine own self.
If I bear witness of myself, my witness is not true.
¶ There is another that beareth witness of me; and I know that the witness which he witnesseth of me is true.
I am one that bear witness of myself, and the Father that sent me beareth witness of me.
And now, behold, you have received a witness; for if I have told you things which no man knoweth have you not received a witness?
¶ If a false witness rise up against any man to testify against him that which is wrong;
Ye yourselves bear me witness, that I said, I am not the Christ, but that I am sent before him.
¶ There is another that beareth witness of me; and I know that the witness which he witnesseth of me is true.
If I bear witness of myself, my witness is not true.
¶ But I have greater witness than that of John: for the works which the Father hath given me to finish, the same works that I do, bear witness of me, that the Father hath sent me.
A false witness shall perish: but the man that heareth speaketh constantly.
Ye sent unto John, and he bare witness unto the truth.
And he said unto them, Unto what then were ye baptized? And they said, Unto John’s baptism.

For John came unto you in the way of righteousness, and ye believed him not: but the publicans and the harlots believed him: and ye, when ye had seen it, repented not afterward, that ye might believe him.
John answered and said, A man can receive nothing, except it be given him from heaven.
And verily, verily, I say unto you, he that receiveth my gospel receiveth me; and he that receiveth not my gospel receiveth not me.
But I receive not testimony from man: but these things I say, that ye might be saved.
May God help us all to live so as to secure and preserve this testimony is my prayer in the name of Jesus. Amen.
Quicken me after thy lovingkindness; so shall I keep the testimony of thy mouth.

But, behold, I have all things as a testimony that these things are true; and ye also have all things as a testimony unto you that they are true; and will ye deny them? Believest thou that these things are true?
Know ye that ye must come unto repentance, or ye cannot be saved.
He was a burning and a shining light: and ye were willing for a season to rejoice in his light.
For perhaps he therefore departed for a season, that thou shouldest receive him for ever;
To him that made great lights: for his mercy endureth for ever:

The lights were immediately extinguished, yet he continued to harangue
them boisterously, until he discovered that his audience had left him.
They seemed to believe what he said, and fled in every direction,
leaving us again to ourselves.
And when the devil had ended all the temptation, he departed from him for a season.

The lights were immediately extinguished, yet he continued to harangue them boisterously, until he discovered that his audience bad left him. They seemed to believe what he said, and fled in every direction, leaving us again to ourselves.
¶ But I have greater witness than that of John: for the works which the Father hath given me to finish, the same works that I do, bear witness of me, that the Father hath sent me.
If we receive the witness of men, the witness of God is greater: for this is the witness of God which he hath testified of his Son.
And the Father himself, which hath sent me, hath borne witness of me. Ye have neither heard his voice at any time, nor seen his shape.
But verily God hath heard me; he hath attended to the voice of my prayer.
But behold, ye have both heard my voice, and seen me; and ye are my sheep, and ye are numbered among those whom the Father hath given me.
And the Spirit said unto me: Believest thou that thy father saw the tree of which he hath spoken?
For we saw him, even on the right hand of God; and we heard the voice bearing record that he is the Only Begotten of the Father—
And ye have not his word abiding in you: for whom he hath sent, him ye believe not.
But if ye believe not his writings, how shall ye believe my words?
And I said unto him: Believest thou the scriptures? And he said, Yea.
And he said: Yea, I believe all the words which thou hast spoken.
Him that is weak in the faith receive ye, but not to doubtful disputations.
Behold, and lo, there are none to deliver you; for ye obeyed not my voice when I called to you out of the heavens; ye believed not my servants, and when they were sent unto you ye received them not.
¶ Search the scriptures; for in them ye think ye have eternal life: and they are they which testify of me.
But blessed are they who are faithful and endure, whether in life or in death, for they shall inherit eternal life.
To others it is given to believe on their words, that they also might have eternal life if they continue faithful.

_Faith and Works_--They who secure eternal life are doers of the
word as well as hearers. 14:37.
And ye will not come to me, that ye might have life.
Ye shall seek me, and shall not find me: and where I am, thither ye cannot come.
But if ye receive me in the world, then shall ye know me, and shall receive your exaltation; that where I am ye shall be also.
That ye may walk honestly toward them that are without, and that ye may have lack of nothing.
Yet a little while, and the world seeth me no more; but ye see me: because I live, ye shall live also.
And thus, if ye will not nourish the word, looking forward with an eye of faith to the fruit thereof, ye can never pluck of the fruit of the tree of life.
I receive not honour from men.
I command and men obey not; I revoke and they receive not the blessing.
That all men should honour the Son, even as they honour the Father. He that honoureth not the Son honoureth not the Father which hath sent him.
Therefore let no man glory in men. For all things are yours;
A man’s pride shall bring him low: but honour shall uphold the humble in spirit.
But I know you, that ye have not the love of God in you.
He that loveth not knoweth not God; for God is love.
But if any man love God, the same is known of him.
Yet in this thing ye did not believe the Lord your God,
But the other of love, knowing that I am set for the defence of the gospel.
I am come in my Father’s name, and ye receive me not: if another shall come in his own name, him ye will receive.
And whatsoever ye shall ask the Father in my name, which is right, believing that ye shall receive, behold it shall be given unto you.
And the Father and I are one. I am in the Father and the Father in me; and inasmuch as ye have received me, ye are in me and I in you.
Whosoever shall receive one of such children in my name, receiveth me: and whosoever shall receive me, receiveth not me, but him that sent me.
Behold, I am Jesus Christ, the Son of God. I came unto mine own, and mine own received me not.
How can ye believe, which receive honour one of another, and seek not the honour that cometh from God only?
Jesus answered, If I honour myself, my honour is nothing: it is my Father that honoureth me; of whom ye say, that he is your God:
Do not think that I will accuse you to the Father: there is one that accuseth you, even Moses, in whom ye trust.
Believest thou not that I am in the Father, and the Father in me? the words that I speak unto you I speak not of myself: but the Father that dwelleth in me, he doeth the works.
And Moses said unto his father in law, Because the people come unto me to inquire of God:
For thy servant became surety for the lad unto my father, saying, If I bring him not unto thee, then I shall bear the blame to my father for ever.
For had ye believed Moses, ye would have believed me: for he wrote of me.
For the Lord had spoken unto Moses, saying,
Thus did Moses: according to all that the Lord commanded him, so did he.
And by him all that believe are justified from all things, from which ye could not be justified by the law of Moses.
And said unto them, Ye have kept all that Moses the servant of the Lord commanded you, and have obeyed my voice in all that I commanded you:
If ye had known me, ye should have known my Father also: and from henceforth ye know him, and have seen him.
But if ye believe not his writings, how shall ye believe my words?
And he that believeth not my words believeth not my disciples; and if it so be that I do not speak, judge ye; for ye shall know that it is I that speaketh, at the last day.
So shall I have wherewith to answer him that reproacheth me: for I trust in thy word.
And ye have not his word abiding in you: for whom he hath sent, him ye believe not.
